refinement_sections.content: Clearly define customer churn in the context of our business, specifying the criteria for determining whether a customer is considered to have churned. Establish the time frame for historical data to be used in the model and clarify the target variable.

Provide a comprehensive overview of the historical sales and interaction data being utilized. Include details on the data sources, variables included, data cleaning processes, and any transformations applied to prepare the data for modeling.

Detail the predictive model being used, including the algorithm(s) employed, hyperparameters set, and the rationale behind the choice of model. Discuss how the model was trained and validated, including any techniques such as cross-validation.

Specify the performance metrics to be used for evaluating the model, such as accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, ROC-AUC, and confusion matrix. Justify the choice of these metrics in relation to the business goals and implications of customer churn.

Outline the methods for detecting overfitting, such as comparing training and validation performance, using learning curves, and applying regularization techniques. Discuss the importance of generalization and how it will be assessed.

Based on the performance analysis, suggest specific methods for improvement, which may include feature engineering, model tuning, ensemble methods, or the use of different algorithms. Provide a rationale for each suggestion.

Define the structure of the final report, ensuring it includes an executive summary, detailed performance analysis, visualizations of key metrics, and a clear presentation of recommendations. Specify the audience for the report and how the findings will be communicated effectively.
